title: Wrong results for queries with "OR" and "LEFT JOIN"
poc: CREATE TABLE test1 (a INT, b INT, d INT);
CREATE TABLE test_dic1 (a INT, c INT);
SELECT dd.* FROM test_dic1 AS dd INNER JOIN test1 AS d1 ON d1.a = dd.a AND d1.d = 1 LEFT JOIN test1 AS d2 ON d2.a = dd.a AND d2.d = 2 WHERE dd.c = 1 OR dd.c = 3;
key_bug_features: Queries with OR and LEFT JOIN produce wrong results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test2 (x INT, y VARCHAR(50), z DATE);
CREATE TABLE test_dic2 (x INT, w FLOAT);
SELECT d2.* FROM test_dic2 AS d2 LEFT JOIN test2 AS t1 ON t1.x = d2.x AND t1.z IS NULL WHERE d2.w > 1.0 OR d2.w IS NULL;
